payload.title: 2025 "The Spellbound Stag" Kunlu Shan Big Tree Bai Cha / White Tea - 200g Cake
payload.body_html:
We went to Kunlu Shan sheng hunting but were impressed with the white teas that we found. We created this blend to highlight the amazing experience we had with these white teas. This is primarily a blend of their big tree white, but there is some old tree material in there as well.

This tea is very floral with blood orange citrus notes and a hint of ripe banana. The color is rich. It is sweet, juicy, and creamy. This is a very clean brewing tea with good flavor and aroma. We're sure it will age well; if you can refrain from drinking it. 

This is a pressed 200g cake. It is a single origin offering that is unique and offers an incredible tea experience. It is from a single garden and remains unblended and processed individually.

Brewing Instructions

Use 5-10 grams of leaves and brew with 75-150ml ( 2.5-5oz ) of water at or near boiling. Rinse once for a few seconds. Start with quick steeps under 10s. With each re-steep adjust the steep time to your taste.

payload.title: 2025 "Dirty Dozen" 12th Anniversary Tuo Cha :: Sheng / Raw Puerh Tea :: Seattle Inventory
payload.body_html:
This tea was twelve years in the making!

This is our 12th year in business. It hasn't always been pretty but we're still hear and we've still got amazing teas for you. This anniversary blend is our most ambitious yet. We took 12 interesting sheng puerh teas we've sourced over the last 12 years and blended them all together into this. This is a very special blend. It perfectly encapsulates our 12 years of Yunnan tea exploration and adventure. 

Grab a tuo of this tea and think of us while you're drinking it. We certainly thought of you while we were making it!

This material is 100 grams pressed into a traditional shape called "tuo".  This is an upside down birds nest shape. It has a lot of surface area in a small shape. Some producers prefer this shape. They believe that air gets trapped in the nest and helps with aging.

 

There are no samples available for this tea.

Brewing Instructions

Use 5-10 grams of leaves and brew with 75-150ml ( 2.5-5oz ) of water at or near boiling. Rinse once for a few seconds. Start with quick steeps under 10s. With each re-steep adjust the steep time to your taste.

payload.title: 2025 "Mountain Shadow" Shou / Ripe Puerh Tea - 200g Cake :: Seattle Inventory
payload.body_html:
The crown of the Cascade Mountains in Washington State is Mt. Rainier. When the conditions are just right the rising or setting sun creates a shadow of Mt, Rainier on the clouds. It is a rare event, but part of the magic and mystery of the Pacific Northwest. This tea is meant to embrace that magic to share with you. 

This is a lovely shou puerh tea blend that really has a magical essence to it. It has your standard shou puerh earthiness but there's something more. 

This is a pressed 200g cake made from a custom blend of shou puerh tea.

Brewing Instructions

Use 5-10 grams of leaves and brew with 75-150ml ( 2.5-5oz ) of water at or near boiling. Rinse once for a few seconds. Start with quick steeps under 10s. With each re-steep adjust the steep time to your taste.

payload.title: 2025 "Rise of the Dark Lotus" Shou / Ripe Puerh Tea - 200g Cake :: Seattle Inventory
payload.body_html:
This tea has been on our mind for a few years. We just needed to find the right material. The very last day we were in Kunming in the Summer of 2025 we found it. We were at our friend's tea shop and she had just one more tea for us to try. She warned us that it was a little weird and she wasn't sure if she liked it or not. From the first taste we knew this was the material we had been looking for.

We took what she had and started experimenting on blending it with some of the other shou we had gathered. It didn't take long to get the ratio right. This tea just wanted to be made. We bought all we could get and had it blended and pressed this fall. 

This tea is fun. It is a super clean and clear brewing tea. It is very smooth and creamy with a pronounced sweetness. This is a very generous tea that will brew for a great many silky smooth steeps. It brews dark. It has some wonderful tobacco earthiness in the aroma with what feels like a purple leaf shou funk without any purple leaf material used. It feels familiar and new at the same time. This is a very special shou that will be popular for a long time.

This is a pressed 200g cake made from a custom blend of shou puerh tea.

Brewing Instructions

Use 5-10 grams of leaves and brew with 75-150ml ( 2.5-5oz ) of water at or near boiling. Rinse once for a few seconds. Start with quick steeps under 10s. With each re-steep adjust the steep time to your taste.

payload.title: "Gourdsmasher" Sheng / Raw Puerh Tea Dragon Balls :: Seattle Inventory
payload.body_html:
Get ready to smash your gourd! This tea is a potent cha qi powerhouse. We had fun with this tea. It's got a classic sheng profile with good bitterness and astringency. It's a strong tea though. It will sneak up on you. This is a fun tea to go hard steeping on with friends.

You can get either 6x or 12x 5g tea balls in one packet. That's 30 or 60 grams of total tea. 5 grams is perfect for a solo session. Add a second ball if you're brewing for friends.

Brewing Instructions

These steep super easy! Drop one ball into your brew vessel of choice 75-100ml or so. Add boiling water slowly. Let it steep for 30s and watch the ball open up. Discard this initial rinse; or drink it if you're super brave! Wait 5 minutes with the lid on and the tea ball will be open and ready to brew. Start with quick steeps under 10s. With each re-steep adjust the steep time to your taste. Enjoy and don't say we didn't warn you! 😜

payload.body_html:

payload.title: 2018 "Dark Depths" Shou / Ripe Puerh :: Seattle Inventory
payload.body_html:
This tea has been a long time coming. We started working on this blend early in 2018. We had a vision for what we wanted and stuck it out until we found exactly the right material to blend. It wasn't an easy hunt. A few of you had a chance to try it loose but we ran out of time to get it pressed last year. The issue really was that we needed the right piece of art.

The missing piece was created by Carl Faulkner ( https://www.instagram.com/visualtaffy/ ) . I've known Carl for a long time. He ran an amazing studio out of the 619 Building in Seattle's Pioneer Square called X17. I loved the place and the art that he and his friends created. I would visit every month during open gallery night. I couldn't get enough. I've followed his work over the years and when we started Crimson Lotus Tea I knew I wanted to work with him on a project somehow. 

Late last year we messaged Carl who lives in Portland now and asked if we could stop by, brew tea for him, and chat about a potential collaboration. We drank tea for hours. It was great. I shared my vision for this tea and Carl was more than happy to help us out.  

Dark Depths is something I've envisioned since our earliest days. The shou puerh that truly captivates me is the super thick, super dark brewing, inky black, raven's wing in the dark of night versions. The ones that offer no reflection and seem to pull in all available light. We missed the chance on a perfect material our second year and have focused hard until we got what we needed for this tea. 

This tea is a monster. It is hiding in the deep waiting to be called forth. It rises in your cup and provides steep after steep of thick, dark, motor oily goodness. 

As good as the loose leaf blend was it turned out 10x better after pressing. This tea is aromatic like the Nannuo Mini Mushrooms and just keeps on going. It is rich and thick and luscious and sweet. It's so much more than most shou puerh. It's herbal and earthy and as deep as the Marianas.

Descend friend and drink from the deep!

"Dark Depths Shou Puer is perfect for those who love their shou with a generously thick texture, sweetness, and balance. It has not a lick of funk, wet pile or fishiness. That said, it is amazing easy drinking. "
--oolongowl.com

Brewing Instructions

Use 5-10 grams of leaves and brew with 75-150ml ( 2.5-5oz ) of water at or near boiling. Rinse once for a few seconds. Start with quick steeps under 10s. With each re-steep adjust the steep time to your taste.

payload.title: "Shou Girls" a 2025 Shou / Ripe Puerh Tea Blend :: Seattle Inventory
payload.body_html:
These four Shou Girls put on a great show! This is a custom blended shou puerh that is super cozy and comforting. It's a very smooth, easy drinking shou. It just feels right. It is a relaxing, daily drinker shou. 

Each wrapper design was painted with watercolor by Seattle artist Stasia Burrington. She is the same artist from our Space Girls cakes. Take the time to explore her amazing artwork: http://www.stasiaburrington.com/

Just like with "Space Girls" each order comes with 4x 50g cakes in a hand wrapped bamboo tong. Each cake is wrapped with a different Shou Girl design. The tea is the same in each cake.

The cakes are heavily pressed and it's easiest to break them into brewable chunks by snapping the cakes with your hands instead of using a tea knife.

We are excited for people to get their own bamboo wrapped tong. Receiving and opening a tong is exciting. Most people don't get the chance to experience opening their own tong. We don't offer samples of this tea because we want everyone to get their own tong to open.

Brewing Instructions

Use 5-10 grams of leaves and brew with 75-150ml ( 2.5-5oz ) of water at or near boiling. Rinse once for a few seconds. Start with quick steeps under 10s. With each re-steep adjust the steep time to your taste.

payload.title: Peanut Teapet :: Seattle Inventory
payload.body_html:
These things are hilarious and highly realistic. We've seen these at tea tables across Yunnan and they are always a treat. You really think they're real. Most tea tables in China have snacks on them already including peanuts so it's funny when people grab these and then realize they're clay.

These are made from natural Chinese clay and have not been glazed. They will absorb the tea oils from your gongfu sessions over time. The more tea you pour over them the more they look like a real peanut.

Read about the history and use of teapets here: Tea pets are the perfect companions on your tea journey!

These each measure approximately 4.5cm x 2cm.
